title: XRF Soil Sample Preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6f8k5","text":"This protocol outlines a procedure for the preparation of both wet and dry soil samples for X-ray Fluorescence (XRF) inorganic chemical analysis. The accurate analysis of soil samples is essential for understanding elemental composition and its implications for various environmental studies. Proper preparation ensures reliable and reproducible results.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"67h43","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"emji0","text":"For wet soil samples, the protocol encompasses drying to remove excess moisture, preventing potential interference with subsequent XRF analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ju93","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3mq3","text":"For wet and dry soil samples, the protocol outlines grinding to ensure homogeneity and fine particle size. Proper grinding enhances the accuracy of XRF analysis by promoting even distribution and facilitating the release of elemental components.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Place soil in a sterile, heat-resistant vessel. Place the vessel on a hotplate at 50 o C until soil is visibly dry (30 min - 1 hr).
Grind with a mortar and pestle until the soil turns to powder (1-2 mins)
Grind with a mortar and pestle until the soil turns to powder (1-2 mins)
